evidence_refs.source_quote: Manhattan Community Board 5

payload.description: Bus priority improvements on 34th Street including repaving and restriping for 5 travel lanes from 3rd Avenue to 9th Avenue, river-to-river curb bus lanes with red lanes, new overhead signage, and extended hours. Implementation by end of 2008.

Long-term concept for a river-to-river fully separated two-way transitway on one side of 34th Street with options for one-way traffic outbound from midtown, one-way traffic westbound, new plaza and pedestrian spaces, and reserved commercial loading space
Bus priority corridor in Manhattan with proposed double bus lane from 23rd to 59th Street. Existing single bus lane from 86th Street to 34th Street.

Crosstown bus priority corridor in Manhattan with improved river-to-river curb bus lanes, red lanes, new overhead signage, and extended hours. Long-term concept for a fully separated two-way transitway.
Bus priority improvements including bus lane conversions, red bus lanes, and overhead signage on Fifth Avenue and Madison Avenue from 23rd Street to 59th Street
Bus priority corridor in Manhattan with proposed double bus lane extended to 23rd Street. Existing double bus lane from 42nd Street to 59th Street.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Corridor serves 6 local bus routes with over 100,000 combined daily riders
over 100,000 combined daily riders
Will improve travel times by up to 20% through the corridor
Crosstown bus service will move faster, benefiting over 15,000 local riders per day
Plan also benefits express buses on 23 routes
Transitway will decrease travel time by up to 45% for crosstown bus service
